title: OptimusKG
description: Data source: Vittor, L. et al. OptimusKG: Unifying biomedical knowledge in a modern multimodal graph. In Review (2026).

latestCypherQueries.query:
CYPHER runtime=parallel
MATCH ()-[n]->()
RETURN type(n) as type, count(n) as qty

MATCH ()-[n]->()
RETURN type(n) as type, count(n) as qty

MATCH (n)
RETURN labels(n)[0] as label, count(n) as qty

MATCH (n)
RETURN labels(n) as label, count(n) as qty

settings.query:
CYPHER runtime=parallel
MATCH ()-[n]->()
RETURN type(n) as type, count(n) as qty
ORDER BY qty DESC

widgets.title: Which intermediate gene connects TP53 to BRCA1 in exactly 2 hops?
widgets.description: Variable-length path between two specific genes — the essence of graph traversal

latestCypherQueries.query:
CYPHER runtime=parallel
MATCH (g1:Gene {symbol: 'TP53'})
      -[:INTERACTS_WITH]-(mid:Gene)
      -[:INTERACTS_WITH]-(g2:Gene {symbol: 'BRCA1'})
RETURN mid.symbol AS intermediate_gene, mid.name AS name
LIMIT 50

CYPHER runtime=parallel
MATCH (g1:Gene {symbol: 'TP53'})
      -[:INTERACTS_WITH]-(mid:Gene)
      -[:INTERACTS_WITH]-(g2:Gene {symbol: 'BRCA1'})
RETURN mid.symbol AS intermediate_gene, mid.name AS name
LIMIT 10

settings.query:
CYPHER runtime=parallel
MATCH (g1:Gene {symbol: 'TP53'})
      -[:INTERACTS_WITH]-(mid:Gene)
      -[:INTERACTS_WITH]-(g2:Gene {symbol: 'BRCA1'})
RETURN mid.symbol AS intermediate_gene, mid.name AS name
LIMIT 50

widgets.title: Which approved drugs target genes shared between cancer and diabetes?
widgets.description: Set intersection via graph — a gene belongs to two disease clusters simultaneously

latestCypherQueries.query:
CYPHER runtime=parallel
MATCH (d1:Disease)-[:ASSOCIATED_WITH]->(g:Gene)<-[:ASSOCIATED_WITH]-(d2:Disease)
WHERE toLower(d1.name) CONTAINS 'cancer'
  AND toLower(d2.name) CONTAINS 'diabetes'
MATCH (drug:Drug)-[:INHIBITOR|ANTAGONIST|AGONIST]->(g)
WHERE drug.is_approved = true
RETURN drug.name, collect(DISTINCT g.symbol) AS genes,
       count(DISTINCT g) AS total
ORDER BY total DESC LIMIT 5

settings.query:
CYPHER runtime=parallel
MATCH (d1:Disease)-[:ASSOCIATED_WITH]->(g:Gene)<-[:ASSOCIATED_WITH]-(d2:Disease)
WHERE toLower(d1.name) CONTAINS 'cancer'
  AND toLower(d2.name) CONTAINS 'diabetes'
MATCH (drug:Drug)-[:INHIBITOR|ANTAGONIST|AGONIST]->(g)
WHERE drug.is_approved = true
RETURN drug.name, collect(DISTINCT g.symbol) AS genes,
       count(DISTINCT g) AS total
ORDER BY total DESC LIMIT 5

widgets.title: Which pesticide exposures are linked to overlapping sets of diseases?
widgets.description: Bipartite graph Exposure → Disease exposes shared disease risk across different chemical agents

latestCypherQueries.query:
CYPHER runtime=parallel
MATCH path=(exp:Exposure)-[:LINKED_TO]->(d:Disease)
WHERE exp.name IN ['Pesticides','Dieldrin','Dichlorvos','Parathion','Dimethoate']
RETURN path

settings.query:
CYPHER runtime=parallel
MATCH path=(exp:Exposure)-[:LINKED_TO]->(d:Disease)
WHERE exp.name IN ['Pesticides','Dieldrin','Dichlorvos','Parathion','Dimethoate']
RETURN path

widgets.title: How do REGORAFENIB, DASATINIB and SORAFENIB connect to cancer types through shared gene targets?
widgets.description: Tripartite graph Drug → Gene → Disease reveals which genes act as therapeutic hubs across multiple cancers

latestCypherQueries.query:
CYPHER runtime=parallel
MATCH path=(drug:Drug)-[r:INHIBITOR|TARGET]->(g:Gene)<-[:ASSOCIATED_WITH]-(d:Disease)
WHERE drug.name IN ['REGORAFENIB','DASATINIB','SORAFENIB']
  AND d.name IN ['breast cancer','lung cancer','leukemia','prostate cancer']
RETURN path

CYPHER runtime=parallel
MATCH path=(g:Gene {symbol: 'TP53'})-[:INTERACTS_WITH]-(n:Gene)
WITH path, g, n LIMIT 15
OPTIONAL MATCH path2=(n)-[:ASSOCIATED_WITH]-(d:Disease)
RETURN path,path2

CYPHER runtime=parallel
MATCH path=(g:Gene {symbol: 'TP53'})-[:INTERACTS_WITH]-(n:Gene)
WITH g, n LIMIT 15
OPTIONAL MATCH path2=(n)-[:ASSOCIATED_WITH]-(d:Disease)
RETURN path,path2

CYPHER runtime=parallel
MATCH (g:Gene {symbol: 'TP53'})-[:INTERACTS_WITH]-(n:Gene)
WITH g, n LIMIT 15
OPTIONAL MATCH (n)-[:ASSOCIATED_WITH]-(d:Disease)
RETURN *

CYPHER runtime=parallel
MATCH (g:Gene {symbol: 'TP53'})-[:INTERACTS_WITH]-(n:Gene)
WITH g, n LIMIT 15
OPTIONAL MATCH (n)-[:ASSOCIATED_WITH]-(d:Disease)
RETURN g.symbol AS center, n.symbol AS gene,
       count(DISTINCT d) AS disease_count

settings.query:
CYPHER runtime=parallel
MATCH path=(drug:Drug)-[r:INHIBITOR|TARGET]->(g:Gene)<-[:ASSOCIATED_WITH]-(d:Disease)
WHERE drug.name IN ['REGORAFENIB','DASATINIB','SORAFENIB']
  AND d.name IN ['breast cancer','lung cancer','leukemia','prostate cancer']
RETURN path

widgets.title: Which environmental exposures are linked to diseases with more than 3 drugs under investigation?
widgets.description: 3-entity chain: Exposure → Disease → Drug, with aggregation filter in the middle

latestCypherQueries.query:
CYPHER runtime=parallel
MATCH (exp:Exposure)-[:LINKED_TO]->(d:Disease)
      <-[:INDICATION]-(drug:Drug)
WITH exp, d, count(DISTINCT drug) AS drug_count
WHERE drug_count > 3
RETURN exp.name AS exposure, d.name AS disease, drug_count
ORDER BY drug_count DESC LIMIT 10

settings.query:
CYPHER runtime=parallel
MATCH (exp:Exposure)-[:LINKED_TO]->(d:Disease)
      <-[:INDICATION]-(drug:Drug)
WITH exp, d, count(DISTINCT drug) AS drug_count
WHERE drug_count > 3
RETURN exp.name AS exposure, d.name AS disease, drug_count
ORDER BY drug_count DESC LIMIT 10

widgets.title: For which disease does one drug have an indication while another is contraindicated?
widgets.description: Therapeutic conflict detection — two opposing relationship directions converging on the same node

latestCypherQueries.query:
CYPHER runtime=parallel
MATCH (drug:Drug)-[:INDICATION]->(disease:Disease)
      <-[:CONTRAINDICATION]-(drug2:Drug)
WHERE drug <> drug2
RETURN disease.name AS disease,
       drug.name AS indicated,
       drug2.name AS contraindicated
LIMIT 8

settings.query:
CYPHER runtime=parallel
MATCH (drug:Drug)-[:INDICATION]->(disease:Disease)
      <-[:CONTRAINDICATION]-(drug2:Drug)
WHERE drug <> drug2
RETURN disease.name AS disease,
       drug.name AS indicated,
       drug2.name AS contraindicated
LIMIT 8

widgets.title: Which genes act as "molecular bridges" between Alzheimer's and Parkinson's?
widgets.description: 2-hop graph traversal (Gene → Gene) anchored simultaneously on two disease sets

latestCypherQueries.query:
CYPHER runtime=parallel
MATCH (d1:Disease) WHERE toLower(d1.name) CONTAINS 'alzheimer'
MATCH (d2:Disease) WHERE toLower(d2.name) CONTAINS 'parkinson'
MATCH path=(d1)-[:ASSOCIATED_WITH]->(g1:Gene)
      -[:INTERACTS_WITH]->(g2:Gene)<-[:ASSOCIATED_WITH]-(d2)
RETURN path
LIMIT 1000

CYPHER runtime=parallel
MATCH (d1:Disease) WHERE toLower(d1.name) CONTAINS 'alzheimer'
MATCH (d2:Disease) WHERE toLower(d2.name) CONTAINS 'parkinson'
MATCH (d1)-[:ASSOCIATED_WITH]->(g1:Gene)
      -[:INTERACTS_WITH]->(g2:Gene)<-[:ASSOCIATED_WITH]-(d2)
RETURN g1.symbol AS gene1, g2.symbol AS gene2
LIMIT 1000

MATCH (d2:Disease) WHERE toLower(d2.name) CONTAINS 'parkinson'
MATCH (d1)-[:ASSOCIATED_WITH]->(g1:Gene)
      -[:INTERACTS_WITH]->(g2:Gene)<-[:ASSOCIATED_WITH]-(d2)
RETURN g1.symbol AS gene1, g2.symbol AS gene2

CYPHER runtime=parallel
MATCH (d1:Disease) WHERE toLower(d1.name) CONTAINS 'alzheimer'
MATCH (d2:Disease) WHERE toLower(d2.name) CONTAINS 'parkinson'
MATCH path=(d1)-[:ASSOCIATED_WITH]->(g1:Gene)
      -[:INTERACTS_WITH]->(g2:Gene)<-[:ASSOCIATED_WITH]-(d2)
RETURN path

CYPHER runtime=parallel
MATCH (d1:Disease) WHERE toLower(d1.name) CONTAINS 'alzheimer'
MATCH (d2:Disease) WHERE toLower(d2.name) CONTAINS 'parkinson'
MATCH (d1)-[:ASSOCIATED_WITH]->(g1:Gene)
      -[:INTERACTS_WITH]->(g2:Gene)<-[:ASSOCIATED_WITH]-(d2)
RETURN g1.symbol AS gene1, g2.symbol AS gene2
LIMIT 10
